context: Tests whether the agent correctly handles pronunciation fixes, normalization flags, language bias, and v3-specific pause/break syntax when scripting sag TTS output. The task involves a technical product announcement with challenging terms, numbers, and URLs.

checklist.name: Respelling used
checklist.description: At least one product name, technical term, or proper noun in the script is respelled (e.g. hyphenated, casing adjusted) to guide pronunciation rather than passed verbatim

checklist.name: Normalize flag present
checklist.description: The sag command includes --normalize auto (or --normalize off with a justification) for text containing numbers, units, or URLs

checklist.name: Language flag present
checklist.description: The sag command includes a --lang flag (e.g. --lang en) to set language bias

checklist.name: v3 pause syntax used
checklist.description: Pauses are expressed using [pause], [short pause], or [long pause] — NOT SSML <break> tags

checklist.name: No SSML break in v3
checklist.description: The script does NOT contain any <break ... /> SSML tags (which are unsupported in eleven_v3)

checklist.name: Audio tags at line start
checklist.description: Any v3 audio style tags (e.g. [excited], [whispers]) appear at the beginning of their line or sentence, not mid-sentence

checklist.name: Correct model reference
checklist.description: Script references eleven_v3 (either as default or explicitly via -m flag) rather than a v2 model when expressive delivery is the goal

checklist.name: ELEVENLABS_API_KEY preferred
checklist.description: Script uses ELEVENLABS_API_KEY environment variable (not SAG_API_KEY) when referencing the API key

checklist.name: Script is executable bash
checklist.description: Output is a self-contained bash script (has shebang or is structured as runnable shell commands) rather than prose instructions

checklist.name: v3 audio tag used
checklist.description: Script uses at least one v3 expressive audio tag (e.g. [excited], [whispers], [shouts]) for the high-energy or emotional sections of the announcement
